channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Massive Exposure of One Billion Identity Records in Unsecured Vendor Database
item.description: A major data exposure was recently discovered involving a database containing roughly one billion sensitive identity records from at least 26 countries. The unsecured repository was linked to IDMerit,...

item.title: German Steel Companies Suspend Energy Transition Projects Amid Economic Crisis
item.description: Since last year, major German steel companies such as ArcelorMittal, Salzgitter, and Thyssenkrupp have postponed or halted their energy transition and CO2 reduction initiatives due to economic pressur...

item.title: NATO Faces Internal Tensions and Blame Over Military Spending
item.description: NATO is currently experiencing a significant internal crisis, with member countries openly criticizing each other over military spending. Poland, acting as a voice for the United States, has publicly ...

item.title: ICE Expands Detention Camps by Converting Warehouses into Immigrant Holding Facilities
item.description: The Trump administration has begun purchasing large industrial warehouses across the U.S. with the intent to convert them into mass detention centers for immigrants. These spaces, typically designed f...

item.title: Sam Altman Highlights the Energy Costs of AI and Human Development
item.description: In a recent interview, Sam Altman, the CEO of OpenAI, compared the resources required to train AI models with the time and energy needed for human development. He emphasized that while there is a lot ...

item.title: Small Business Owner Who Supported Trump Pleads for the Release of Detained Fiancée Facing Deportation
item.description: Wayne DeMario, a Miami-Dade small business owner, is making an emotional plea to President Donald Trump after his fiancée of 20 years, Yamile Alcantu, was detained by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enfo...

item.title: Millie Bobby Brown Marks 22nd Birthday with David Harbour Amid Past Controversy
item.description: Millie Bobby Brown recently celebrated her 22nd birthday at Maison Close in New York City with a special group of guests, including her husband Jake Bongiovi, 'Stranger Things' co-star David Harbour, ...

item.title: North Korea Showcases New Multiple-Launch Missile System with Potential Strategic Capabilities
item.description: North Korea has unveiled a new missile launcher system capable of firing multiple projectiles, according to an announcement made by leader Kim Jong-un and later broadcast by the state media outlet KCN...

item.title: Investigation Reveals Russian General's Involvement in Torture and Killing of Ukrainian POWs
item.description: An investigation by Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty has uncovered private messages, photos, and videos implicating Russian General Roman Demurchiev in war crimes against Ukrainian prisoners of war (PO...

item.title: Billionaire Criticism of Spain's Prime Minister Highlights Success in Workers' Rights and Economic Growth
item.description: When Elon Musk recently branded Spain’s Prime Minister, Pedro Sánchez, a 'true fascist totalitarian', it sparked questions about why billionaires seem to despise his leadership. Sánchez has implemente...

item.title: Leqaa Kordia's Struggle in ICE Custody: A Year of Neglect and Cruelty
item.description: Leqaa Kordia, a 33-year-old Palestinian woman, has been detained in an ICE facility for almost a year. Arrested after protesting for Palestinian rights, she has faced severe medical neglect and inhuma...

item.title: New Jersey Man With No Criminal Record Detained by ICE After Years-Old Deportation Order
item.description: Abdellatif Hafraoui, a 60-year-old Moroccan immigrant who has lived in the United States for nearly four decades, was detained by ICE for 108 days after being arrested at Newark Liberty International ...

item.title: Supreme Court Invalidates Trump’s Emergency Tariffs, $175 Billion Could Be Refunded
item.description: The Supreme Court of the United States has ruled that former President Donald Trump exceeded his authority by imposing sweeping emergency tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ...

item.title: Tucker Carlson Criticizes Israel as the Most Violent Region, Expresses Concerns About Safety During Visit
item.description: Tucker Carlson, the American podcaster and former Fox News host, recently sparked controversy with statements on his podcast, calling Israel 'the most violent region in the world.' Carlson described h...

item.title: U.S. Expands Immigrant Detention Capacity Amid Rising Deportations
item.description: The United States is accelerating plans to expand its immigrant detention centers under the Trump administration. The government is exploring the creation of new facilities and increasing the capacity...

item.title: The Gleiwitz Radio Tower: The Site of a Fake Attack That Triggered WWII
item.description: The Gleiwitz radio station, located near the German-Polish border, is known for being the site of a dramatic incident that led to the outbreak of World War II. On August 31, 1939, Nazi operatives stag...

item.title: Drones Revolutionize Winter Olympics Coverage with Dynamic, Close-Up Action Shots
item.description: The 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan and Cortina marked a turning point in sports broadcasting with the introduction of FPV drones, offering an immersive experience for viewers. Unlike traditional, dista...

item.title: Mullvad's Anti-Censorship Ads Banned on UK Television Amid Rising Surveillance
item.description: Mullvad, a VPN provider, faced censorship in the United Kingdom after attempting to broadcast its TV commercial 'And Then?', directed by Jonas Åkerlund, which criticized mass surveillance and censorsh...

item.title: Connections Between High-Profile PR Firms, Johnny Depp, and Justin Baldoni Highlighted in Epstein Files
item.description: Recent scrutiny of the Jeffrey Epstein files has revealed indirect connections between high-profile celebrities Johnny Depp and Justin Baldoni and crisis publicists previously linked to Epstein. Depar...

item.title: UK Couple Receives $200,000 Medical Bill Following Premature Birth in the US
item.description: UK folk musicians Issy Ferris and Archie Sylvester faced a $200,000 medical bill after their son, Lucky, was born prematurely at 33 weeks during their tour in Nashville. Issy experienced severe sympto...
